name: Essex (United Kingdom)

colors.symbolism: the gold of the heraldic seaxes

description: A red field bearing three gold seaxes (notched Saxon swords) arranged horizontally with the blades pointing upward.
history: The arms of the historic Kingdom of Essex (c. 527–825) and later the Essex County Council; recognised as the county flag and registered with the Flag Institute on 19 July 2013.

features.symbolism: the Saxons of Essex; the curved notch on the blade is the eponymous 'seax' from which the East Saxons took their name
